tweets.text: At the 10th Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons Review Conference, August 1-26, the U.S. will work with partners to strengthen the NPT’s pillars of nonproliferation, disarmament, and promotion of peaceful uses of nuclear technology. More: https://t.co/WXodmZVskT

The Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty is critical to international security and the rules-based international order. In this DipNote blog, @USNPT shares how the U.S. and our partners will work to strengthen the #NPT at its Review Conference next month: https://t.co/zmR9jCsljh.
This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to donate 1.7 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to Djibouti, Lesotho, Sudan, Tanzania, and Somalia. https://t.co/n3IrLcQfyt
Happy #AfricanWomensDay! Today we recognize and celebrate all African women, on the continent and around the globe, who are making the world more peaceful and secure. 🌍

In 1985, then Secretary George Shultz founded the Overseas Security Advisory Council to keep U.S. organizations and personnel around the world connected with @StateDeptDSS and with one another. For 37 years @OSACstate has worked to keep our private-sector partners overseas safe.
Today as we celebrate #AfricasWomensDay we recognize the women in Zambia who have built sustainable businesses and made real differences in their communities. Read more here: https://t.co/zQWovcb3j4.

Contrary to Moscow’s disinformation, sanctions against Russia are not responsible for increasing food and nutrition insecurity worldwide. The Kremlin is. More: https://t.co/nBNQTyWDxe. https://t.co/kQeDI1EqIU

Traveling abroad soon? Before you pack your bags, be sure to check out the State Department’s latest travel advisories for your destination. Follow @travelgov and visit https://t.co/G3AYMssu8v for more. 🧳✈️ https://t.co/xaScxM4hXZ

.@SecBlinken: Moscow has been cherry-picking economic data to support President Putin’s insistence that everything is fine and the Russian economy is going strong. It’s simply not true. https://t.co/ORN76HNEwB

.@StateEUR’s Molly Montgomery @ForeignPressCtr: We will continue working together with our allies and partners, delivering on our unwavering, unified commitment to support Ukraine and build on our unprecedented sanctions, to hold President Putin and his enablers accountable. https://t.co/gmIevRf2gk
Sign up to receive "From the Desk of Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken," email updates from the Secretary on key foreign affairs issues. https://t.co/Rgm7xzIXJL https://t.co/WUJqGHFs4B

.@SecBlinken: The Kremlin says that global businesses haven’t really pulled out of Russia. In fact, more than 1,000 foreign companies – representing assets and revenue equal to more than a third of Russia’s GDP – have stopped operations in Russia. More: https://t.co/epqJnmbmUk https://t.co/RIT874Hrle

On World Day Against Trafficking in Persons, the U.S. stands in solidarity with all those around the world working to #EndHumanTrafficking. Read the Department's latest Trafficking in Persons report, detailing the actions we are taking: https://t.co/GMDRTGwIkX @jtip_state https://t.co/4tPgLu8gQN
This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX to donate 1.7 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to Djibouti, Lesotho, Sudan, Tanzania, and Somalia. https://t.co/WDuJS4YR9A

This month @NMADmuseum hosted a professional development workshop for teachers to share best practices for preparing their students for college, careers, and civic life with the skills of diplomacy. Read more: https://t.co/6pyQ2iUQtz

On July 29, join @PEPFAR and @hhsgov for an informative session to discuss insights and innovations from U.S. gov't #HIV global and domestic programs, highlight opportunities & challenges, and advance shared learning to improve program design, execution, and outcomes. #AIDS2022

The NPT is the basis for international cooperation to prevent the spread of nuclear weapons and work toward their elimination. The United States remains committed to leading the way on arms control and addressing complex global security challenges. https://t.co/OEMLtWsQpo https://t.co/Qg6KxswelT

From July 29 - Aug. 3, @PEPFAR  & @HIVGov  are co-sponsoring the U.S. government delegation to the 24th International @AIDS_conference in Montreal, Canada. The U.S. remains deeply committed to ending the HIV/AIDS epidemic at home & worldwide. #EndAIDS2030 https://t.co/eaiRRO5RFf

Today is #InternationalTigerDay. The U.S. is a leader in the fight against wildlife trafficking. We take a whole-of-government approach to strengthen law enforcement, reduce demand for illegal wildlife products, and build international cooperation to combat wildlife trafficking. https://t.co/lLbVoiKlhM

.@SecBlinken: We’ll continue to stand with Ukraine, support its ability to defend itself, impose costs on Russia until it ends its aggression. We continue to coordinate closely with allies and partners. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/Bp0S4i8N2K
.@SecBlinken: The U.S.-Japan Alliance has never been stronger or more necessary. 🇺🇸 🇯🇵 https://t.co/D4oR6dpb2k

The U.S.-Japan Alliance has served as the cornerstone of peace, security, and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific and across the world for over six decades, and we are committed to working together on shared challenges. https://t.co/PNK196QOaO

Live now! @secblinken and @secraimondo co-host the inaugural ministerial meeting of the U.S.-Japan Economic Policy Consultative Committee with @MofaJapan_en Hayashi and @METI_JPN Haguida. Also streaming on https://t.co/AhrSyM5rNh.  https://t.co/gpQE625FnM

This week, the United States partnered with #COVAX to ship 4,800 pediatric Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Grenada. With these vaccines, Grenadian children will be safer and recovery will continue. https://t.co/4C79lsPWDh

This week, the U.S. delivered 400,140 Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Tajikistan in partnership with #COVAX. Our total vaccine donation to the Tajik people now reaches 3.6 million. We're Tajikistan's proud partner on vaccination efforts, which is vital for global health. https://t.co/FpNFjzqFX0

It’s National Intern Day! Interns are an integral part of our work -- and we're now offering paid @doscareers internships at U.S. embassies and consulates throughout the world, as well as in Washington, D.C. and across the United States.

Every day the Department’s @SciDiplomacyUSA office is advancing efforts to support our planet. For #WorldConservationDay, check out some of the work we are doing to provide clean water, food, and medicine around the globe: https://t.co/j005r2ubKE. https://t.co/NaCCFSuElV

.@SecBlinken: Though the Kremlin is working hard to paint a picture of economic stability, the facts show otherwise. The powerful impact of sanctions will grow and compound over time. https://t.co/dbBji0J15P

.@SecBlinken: In the coming days, I expect to speak with Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov for the first time since before the war began. I plan to raise an issue that’s a top priority for us: the release of Americans Paul Whelan and Brittney Griner. https://t.co/Gvd9gbB5sU
.@SecBlinken: Ukraine has not and will not be conquered. It will remain sovereign and independent. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/C1SFLSxueJ

Established as the first executive agency on July 27, 1789, to lead our nation’s foreign policy, 233 years later the State Department continues our mission of promoting democratic values and the interests of the American people around the world. https://t.co/HUJh1nlphV

Julia from Ukraine, and living in Poland, reflects on Ukrainian identity and the role of the Orange Revolution (2004) in defining what that means today. #DemocracyAllies #StandWithUkraine https://t.co/k3gtjp8x5Q

.@SecBlinken marks the #ADA32 anniversary by visiting the Department’s Access Center: “One of the things that has been hugely important to me is making sure that we are genuinely building a Department that reflects the entirety of our country.” #AccessForAll https://t.co/8w1PArRiiy

As we celebrate 32 years of the Americans with Disabilities Act, Department interns reflect on their experience working through the Workforce Recruitment Program, a referral initiative for college students and recent graduates with disabilities. https://t.co/XzxANt4xdM

.@StateDeptSpox: Today, we celebrate the 32nd anniversary of the Americans with Disabilities Act—the world’s first comprehensive civil rights law for persons with disabilities. Every day, we remain committed to promoting disability rights at home and abroad. #AccessForAll https://t.co/j7JKkDM0zL
Live now! @statedeptspox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/TACmVMIcSf.  https://t.co/VREJT5wfvM

As we commemorate the signing of the Americans with Disabilities Act, we recognize American diplomacy is better because of the contributions persons with disabilities have made at home and around the world. #AccessForAll https://t.co/gRtCLRDX4I

Today marks #ADA32, the anniversary of the U.S. civil rights law prohibiting discrimination against individuals with disabilities. We continue to promote disability rights globally, so those in other countries can enjoy the same rights. #AccessforAll https://t.co/t6MRAopAz2

32 years ago this week, the Americans with Disabilities Act was signed into law. We are proud to celebrate the contributions employees with disabilities make to the Department’s mission. When employees have equal opportunities in the workplace, workforces are stronger. https://t.co/Kk98qbUoVn

This week we commemorate the signing of the Americans with Disabilities Act, which prohibits discrimination against individuals with disabilities in all areas of public life. Employees with disabilities bring skills and experiences to the Department that advance our mission.

.@StateDeptSpox: We strongly condemn the Burmese military’s executions of pro-democracy activists and elected leaders. We call on the regime to cease executions, release all those unjustly detained, and restore Burma’s path to democracy. https://t.co/qZLKjcBco0

America is leading the world on addressing the climate crisis and we will continue on this path. If we succeed, we will capitalize on the greatest opportunity to create quality jobs in generations, build a more equitable, healthy, and sustainable society, and protect our planet. https://t.co/vxi98dJVxx

Only one candidate for @itu Secretary-General embodies that commitment and possesses an ambitious vision of connectivity for all. Doreen Bogdan-Martin is the best candidate to be elected to lead this vital international organization. learn more here: https://t.co/FGM0E9iUVd

With 2.9 billion people still unconnected, now is a pivotal moment for @itu and the push for global connectivity.  It is vital to have strong leadership committed to innovation, collaboration, and partnership to open doors of opportunity to billions around the world.  #Doreen4SG https://t.co/eqCyj5AGwU

.@ITU is responsible for facilitating connectivity around the globe, closing digital divides, harmonizing global telecommunication networks, supporting the multi-trillion dollar telecommunications industry, and contributing to international maritime and aviation safety.

Over the coming weeks, you’ll be hearing a lot about the International Telecommunication Union (@ITU) and the future of global connectivity. In September, members of @itu will meet in Bucharest to elect new leadership, including the next Secretary-General. #Doreen4SG

.@POTUS: I will do everything in my power to clean our air and water, protect our people’s health, to win the clean energy future. If we don’t keep it below 1.5 degrees Centigrade, we lose it all. We don’t get to turn it around. https://t.co/vzzNbuZMFe

In "The Week at State," @SecBlinken co-hosts a virtual COVID-19 Global Action Plan Foreign Ministerial Meeting and a virtual Supply Chain Ministerial Forum and releases the 2022 Trafficking in Persons (TIP) Report. For the latest, visit https://t.co/T9DBinbAPV. https://t.co/PdljkAXlre

Have you read the latest on our official blog, DipNote? Check out the newest entries on this week's TIP Report release and how countries are preparing for the upcoming Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) Review Conference. More below! https://t.co/87Rfebg8BB
.@SecBlinken at the Supply Chain Ministerial: There’s only one way to build supply chains that are resilient, and that’s to do it together, with partners. More: https://t.co/wdUs3nQAbW

.@SecBlinken: To prevent a climate catastrophe and adapt to the growing effects of climate change, we need resilient supply chains to produce clean energy technologies—from wind turbines to batteries. https://t.co/93JeFHYMJv

This year’s High-Level Political Forum on Sustainable Development Review of Sustainable Development Goal 5 on gender equality demonstrated that there is much progress to be made. Read about U.S. efforts to advance gender equity & equality from @StateGWI: https://t.co/DbS7QvJTns

In partnership with the Honduran Government, @usembassyhn hosted a roundtable on gender-based violence and women’s rights in 2021. The roundtable was a major step forward in achieving #WPSinAction strategic priorities. Learn more in the #WPS2022 Report. https://t.co/Sb7h6PtMS4

The U.S. joined in the G7+ Working Group on Women, Peace, & Security and in 2021 was involved in the @UN_Women and Japan-coordinated effort to assist Sri Lanka in developing a #WPSinAction National Action Plan. Learn more in the #WPS2022 Report.

.@USEmbassyCairo held six monthly sessions with senior leadership through the Providing Opportunities for Women’s Economic Rise (POWER) initiative for early and mid-career Egyptian women focused on overcoming corporate, social, and cultural barriers. #WPSinAction

Assistant Secretary for Consular Affairs Rena Bitter explains the State Department’s Travel Advisory system and how @travelgov helps U.S. citizens stay informed about safety & security risks while traveling & living overseas. Check out our latest DipNote: https://t.co/E3KWP1uXsI

@StateGWI @USIP @usembassytokyo In Hungary and Croatia, @StateDeptPM leads the Global Defense Reform Program, which integrated women into trainings focused on building capacity in joint operations planning and countering disinformation to target terrorist radicalization and violent extremism. #WPSinAction

@StateGWI @USIP .@usembassytokyo led several collaborative discussions with major Japanese leaders from the public and private sectors to advance women’s leadership and participation in all sectors of life. Learn more about #WPSinAction efforts in the #WPS2022 Report.

Through #SistersWithoutBorders, @StateGWI  and @USIP supported a network of women leaders in East Africa to create and implement activities to counter violent extremism (CVE) & advocate for women’s leadership & Women, Peace & Security strategies in Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda.

Women, Peace, and Security is a top priority for the Department, and we are taking today to highlight the #WPSinAction initiatives here at home and abroad. Follow along for more: https://t.co/UzGUbNGRed

This week we released the 2022 Women, Peace, and Security Report in collaboration with @deptofdefense, @usaid, and @dhsgov. This report covers our efforts to implement #WPSinAction across the four agencies in FY 2021. Read more: https://t.co/Sb7h6PtMS4.

Every country has a role to play to #EndTrafficking. The annual TIP Report provides a comprehensive review of anti-trafficking efforts globally, objectively assessing 188 governments, including the United States, and their commitment to combat all forms of human trafficking. https://t.co/srxTkncKJH

The United States and Japan co-hosted today's #COVIDMinisterial to sustain global political engagement to end the acute phase of the pandemic and improve global health security. The fight against COVID-19 is not over. Learn more: https://t.co/yPuYqZNrHZ

#TIPReportHero Kateryna Cherepakha engages the Ukrainian government and international stakeholders to build the capacity of officials to identify survivors of human trafficking. In addition, she also conducts training courses for first responders as an @OSCE National Expert. https://t.co/1DTofcguNj

#TIPReportHero Apinya Tajit advocates for workers exploited in forced labor across SE Asia, particularly in the fishing & seafaring industries, assisting survivors with reintegration into society, and sharing her expertise with gov't officials and anti-trafficking authorities. https://t.co/Auu0UDPw66

#TIPReportHero Irena Dawid-Olczyk has assisted survivors of trafficking for more than a quarter of a century, lending her expertise in the creation of anti-trafficking training materials & films. She helps prevent the exploitation of refugees across the Poland-Ukraine border. https://t.co/toy9AHk8Ny

#TIPReportHero Cornelius Wennah helps build capacity in Liberia’s criminal justice sector to successfully prosecute human traffickers. He advocates for the active inclusion of traditional leaders and civil society organizations in Liberia’s National Anti-Trafficking Task Force. https://t.co/CNFBynGo9d

#TIPReportHero Major Mohammad al-Khlaifat implements new ways to cooperate with the anti-trafficking community in Jordan. The impact:  formalized information sharing that improves anti-trafficking efforts and offers survivors vital services. #EndHumanTrafficking https://t.co/MRyDOkbAvP

We are proud to honor #TIPReportHero Mohammed Tariqul Islam, who provides assistance to survivors of human trafficking, helps improve the investigation and prosecution capacity of the Bangladeshi government, and increases cross-border collaboration and coordination. https://t.co/QTjG8WLwtm
